000001 | Scope: evidence-backed technical history from Phase 1 through the approved Phase 7.2A commit; private conversations and private operational data are excluded.
000002 | Project purpose: coordinate marketplace catalog, marking references, customer orders, consignments, staged warehouse work, scanning, assignment, problems, and packing.
000003 | Non-goal: Product Inventory is marketplace catalog data and is not physical stock, purchasing, accounting, or an ERP ledger.
000004 | Release rule: automated approval does not replace responsive browser, sanitized warehouse, backup/restore, and stronger-hardware performance gates.

010581 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CATALOG_ENRICHMENT.md:1 records: # Amazon Catalog Enrichment

Category/product catalogs enrich title, brand, category, subcategory, material, color, size, model, description, bullet points, and safe images. Blank incoming values never erase existing listing data.
010583 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CATALOG_ENRICHMENT.md:5 records: At activation, worker-facing fields are copied into `ConsignmentCatalogSnapshotV1`. The snapshot is bounded, versioned, safe-URL validated, and immutable. It contains source record IDs for audit provenance, never managed paths or raw workbook rows.
010584 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CATALOG_ENRICHMENT.md:7 records: `syncAmazonListings` also returns a typed enrichment result keyed by listing ID. Snapshots use listing identity first, then authoritative operational catalog values, then shipment-row fallback. Material, color, size, model, descriptions, bullets, and secondary images therefore remain available to marking workers without reopening a workbook. Galleries combine listing and catalog images, deduplicate them, and remain capped at ten.

010587 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_ACTIVATION.md:3 records: Only an owner/consignment manager can activate a reviewed Amazon draft. Every line must have a positive quantity, explicit listing match, supported process route, and no unresolved blocking issue.
010588 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_ACTIVATION.md:5 records: `PICK_PACK` creates `PICK READY` and `PACK LOCKED`. `PICK_MARK_PACK` creates `PICK READY`, `MARK LOCKED`, and `PACK LOCKED`. Marking lines require an active linked asset plus instructions or a Master Design ID; missing product images require explicit owner review. Missing worker marking files do not block activation.
010589 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_ACTIVATION.md:7 records: Activation rechecks relationships transactionally, creates one task plan, and freezes identifiers/catalog display data. It never changes inventory or marketplace quantities.

010591 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_IMPORT.md:3 records: Select an active Amazon seller account, open **Owner > Consignments > New**, enter the shipment reference, and upload the shipment report plus optional All Listings/catalog files. ZIP archives may contain the same supported report types.
010592 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_IMPORT.md:5 records: The importer classifies content from headers, stores files in ignored managed storage, synchronizes the selected account's listing master, and creates a draft preview. It never creates worker tasks during upload. If no shipment report or more than one shipment candidate is found, activation remains blocked for owner review.
010593 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_IMPORT.md:7 records: When multiple shipment files or worksheets are detected, the owner chooses one exact stored file and table. Reparsing reuses every stored All Listings/catalog report, clears the previous current-source marker, and rebuilds draft lines without duplicating file records. Only the selected table creates work quantities.
010594 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_IMPORT.md:9 records: Accepted formats: CSV, TSV, TXT, XLSX, XLSM, and ZIP. Legacy XLS, encrypted workbooks, executable entries, nested archives, unsafe paths, and files beyond configured limits are rejected. Never commit real reports, extracted catalogs, or managed import storage.
010595 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:1 records: # Amazon File Classification
010596 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:3 records: Classification is signature-based:
010597 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:5 records: - `AMAZON_SHIPMENT`: shipment reference, positive quantity, and at least one exact product identifier.
010598 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:6 records: - `AMAZON_ALL_LISTINGS`: Seller SKU plus ASIN/FNSKU and listing status.
010599 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:7 records: - `AMAZON_CATEGORY_CATALOG`: product identity, title, category, and enrichment fields.
010600 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:8 records: - `AMAZON_PRODUCT_CATALOG`: product identity, title, and enrichment fields.
010601 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:9 records: - `AMAZON_SUPPORTING`: useful identifiers/enrichment without a stronger profile.
010602 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:10 records: - `UNKNOWN_SUPPORTING`: safe fallback that cannot generate work.
010603 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:12 records: Aliases normalize case, spacing, underscores, dots, and hyphens. A filename or a lone generic `SKU` header cannot make a file a shipment report.
010604 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_FILE_CLASSIFICATION.md:14 records: For Amazon category templates, the parser reads `attributeRow`, `labelRow`, and `dataRow` metadata when present. Otherwise it scores the first 30 rows and prefers machine attribute names such as `item_sku`, `item_name`, `feed_product_type`, and `main_image_url`. Template, Upload, Product, and Inventory sheets outrank Instructions, Data Definitions, Valid Values, Images, Examples, and Dropdowns.
010605 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:1 records: # Amazon Listing Matching
010606 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:3 records: Matches are exact and limited to the selected account plus `AMAZON` marketplace. Priority is:
010607 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:6 records: 2. Seller SKU
010608 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:8 records: 4. External ID
010609 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:9 records: 5. EAN, UPC, or GTIN
010610 | Evidence catalogue docs/AMAZON_LISTING_MATCHING.md:11 records: One unique result is selected. If identifiers point to different listings, the row is `IDENTIFIER_CONFLICT`. If the highest-priority identifier maps to multiple listings, it is `EXACT_MULTIPLE`. Missing identifiers remain `NOT_FOUND`. Titles are display data only and are never automatic match keys.
010611 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:1 records: # Concurrency QA
010612 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:3 records: The workflow uses guarded expected state, assignment checks, request fingerprints, unique action-log keys, and transactional writes.
010613 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:5 records: Phase 7 found that high duplicate SQLite contention could surface socket timeouts. The shared task service now:
010614 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:7 records: - checks idempotent replays without opening an unnecessary read transaction;
010615 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:8 records: - retries transient lock, socket-timeout, transaction-conflict, and unique-replay races with bounded backoff;
010616 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:9 records: - returns a safe `Work is busy; retry the action.` message after exhaustion.
010617 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONCURRENCY_QA.md:11 records: Automated duplicate request levels 2, 5, 10, and 20 mutate quantity once and create one action log. Existing suites separately cover competing claims, customer shipment packing, assembly claim/complete/problem replay, activation replay, payload mismatch, cross-worker request IDs, and removed-account rejection.
010618 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PACKING.md:1 records: # Consignment Packing
010619 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PACKING.md:3 records: Amazon and Flipkart use the same selected-account packing queue. Pack remains locked until prior Pick/Mark stages complete. Final line completion reconciles the generic consignment batch; no inventory or marketplace quantity is changed.
010620 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PACKING.md:5 records: Open `/work/consignments/pack`. Only active READY or IN_PROGRESS PACK tasks appear, and earlier stages must have completed before PACK becomes ready. 010621 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PACKING.md:7 records: Completing final PACK timestamps the line. Central reconciliation marks the batch COMPLETED only after every final PACK task is complete. Completed tasks disappear from active search but remain available under completed history.
010622 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PACKING.md:9 records: This stage is separate from customer-order AWB packing. A universal cross-order and cross-account scanner remains a later phase.
010623 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PICKING.md:1 records: # Consignment Picking
010624 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PICKING.md:3 records: Flipkart and Amazon activated lines share this queue and the same guarded task mutation service. Amazon cards use immutable Seller SKU, ASIN, FNSKU, title/image, account, shipment, route, and exact-match data. Completing Pick unlocks Pack for `PICK_PACK` or Mark for `PICK_MARK_PACK`.

Exact Seller SKU, internal SKU, FSN, listing ID, LID, EAN, UPC, GTIN, and barcode matches are tried before title or consignment text search.
010626 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PICKING.md:7 records: `Start`, `+1`, `+5`, set quantity, and complete remaining are explicit actions. Search or scan never changes quantity. Required quantity is work quantity only; this feature has no stock balance, reservation, or deduction.
010627 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSIGNMENT_PICKING.md:9 records: Completing PICK unlocks only the next stage: PACK for `PICK_PACK`, or MARK for `PICK_MARK_PACK`.
010628 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSINGMENT_TASK_ACTIVATION.md:1 records: # Consignment Task Activation
010629 | Evidence catalogue docs/CONSINGMENT_TASK_ACTIVATION.md:3 records: Activation is an explicit owner or canManageConsignments operation. The server claims only a READY_TO_ACTIVATE selected-account batch, then validates and writes everything in one transaction.

For PICK_MARK_PACK, it creates PICK READY, MARK LOCKED, then PACK LOCKED. Every task carries the same required work quantity. Unique source-stage and source-sequence indexes plus the guarded status claim make double-click and stale activation idempotent.

It rejects LOCKED/PROBLEM tasks, negative/non-integer/over-limit values, and cross-account or unauthorized mutations. Completion unlocks only the next sequence. Scanning alone never mutates a task.

010668 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:1 records: # Owner-only fresh-start database reset
010669 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:3 records: ## Purpose and safety boundary
010670 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:5 records: This checkpoint removes active marketplace and workflow data while retaining one explicitly selected active OWNER, the SQLite schema, and `_prisma_migrations`. It does not delete or recreate `prisma/dev.db`. Implementation and automated tests operate on disposable copies; the real reset is a separate, guarded owner action.
010671 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:7 records: Stop the Next.js server, imports, workers, and every database writer before backup, reset, or restore. Never use `prisma migrate reset`, `prisma db push`, or seeding against the private database.
010672 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:9 records: ## Preserved state
010673 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:11 records: - Exactly the OWNER matched by the exact `--owner-username` argument.
010674 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:12 records: - The owner's ID, username, password hash, name, OWNER role, active state, and `mustChangePassword` state.
010675 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:13 records: - SQLite tables, indexes, constraints, triggers, and applied Prisma migration rows.
010676 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:14 records: - Timestamped database/storage backups and private manifests.
010677 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:15 records: - Source, migrations, documentation, and historical backups.
010678 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:17 records: The reset clears the owner's account selection and assignments, failed-login count, lock, last-login time, IP, and user agent. The password hash is compared byte-for-byte without being printed.
010679 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:19 records: ## Deleted state
010680 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:21 records: Every application table discovered from `sqlite_master` is classified at runtime. `User` preserves only the selected owner and `_prisma_migrations` is preserved in full; all other application tables are emptied in foreign-key-safe order. This includes sessions, password resets, other users, accounts and assignments, listings and identifiers, mappings and caches represented in tables, rules, marking records, orders and problems, uploads and previews, import jobs and issues, consignments and files
010681 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:25 records: ## Inspect and select the owner
010682 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:27 records: ```powershell
010683 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:28 records: npm.cmd run fresh-db:inspect -- --owner-username "YOUR_EXACT_OWNER_USERNAME"
010684 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:31 records: Inspection is read-only. It refuses a missing, blank, inactive, non-OWNER, duplicate, or password-hash-less selection. Output contains usernames and roles, not password hashes, sessions, reset tokens, customer rows, or raw private data. Review the machine-derived table inventory, counts, foreign keys, migration rows, integrity, and active-storage totals.
010685 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:33 records: ## Create and verify the backup
010686 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:35 records: ```powershell
010687 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:36 records: npm.cmd run fresh-db:backup -- --owner-username "YOUR_EXACT_OWNER_USERNAME"
010688 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:39 records: The command creates a non-overwriting directory under `backups/fresh-start/<timestamp>/` containing the consistent SQLite backup, manifest, pre-reset counts, reset plan, and storage backup. The manifest records hashes, sizes, integrity, foreign keys, commit, migration rows, selected owner identity, storage totals, and row-count summary. It deliberately excludes the password hash and raw business rows.

010697 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:57 records: The command refuses unless the manifest and backup hash verify, disposable proof passes, the real database hash still equals the pre-backup source hash, the owner and migrations still match, and the exact flag is present. 010700 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:65 records: A Y/N response is insufficient. After the transaction it checks counts, owner identity, password-hash equality, migrations, integrity, and foreign keys; optimizes and vacuums only after successful checks; writes the private post-reset report; and clears backed-up active storage.

010703 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:71 records: ## Restore warning and procedure
010704 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:73 records: Restore replaces newer data. Stop all writers, identify the exact private manifest, and run:
010705 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:75 records: ```powershell
010706 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:76 records: npm.cmd run fresh-db:restore -- --backup-manifest "backups/fresh-start/TIMESTAMP/backup-manifest.json"
010707 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md:79 records: Type `RESTORE dev.db FROM VERIFIED BACKUP`. The helper verifies the manifest SHA-256, makes a pre-restore safety copy beside the selected backup, restores the database, and checks integrity and foreign keys. It never silently rolls back. Storage restoration is intentionally manual so the owner can decide whether replacing newer files is appropriate; restore matching `storage-backup` contents only while writers remain stopped.

010709 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:1 records: # Fresh-start synthetic QA plan
010710 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:3 records: ## Isolation and data design
010711 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:5 records: Real `prisma/dev.db` remains owner-only after an authorized reset. Full workflow QA belongs in ignored disposable databases under `.codex-tmp`. Synthetic identities must be visibly fake: owner, picker, marker, assembler, packer, view-all worker, and manager; synthetic Flipkart/Amazon accounts; fake listings and identifiers; fake orders, consignments, tasks, problems, and history. Never copy customer, account, SKU, order, AWB, image, session, or token data from private files.
010712 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:7 records: The reset unit fixture applies every SQLite migration and creates synthetic owner/worker/account/assignment/session/reset/audit/listing rows. It verifies exact owner selection, refusal of missing/non-owner selection, transaction purge, unchanged password hash and migrations, cleared account/session/security state, zero operational counts, integrity, foreign keys, and real-command refusal without its confirmation flag.
010713 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:9 records: ## Automated command matrix
010714 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:11 records: Run `prisma:validate`, `typecheck`, `lint`, validator tests, marking/consignment/workflow migration smokes, consignment, workflow, universal scan, assembly, Amazon consignment, product-inventory import, permission, security, concurrency, real-database safety, fresh-database safety, small performance/query-plan tests, history validation, audit, diff check, and a production build. All application suites must use synthetic or disposable data.
010715 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:13 records: Customer workflow scenarios: Pick to Pack; Pick to Assembly to Pack; problem reporting and resolution; multi-item Flipkart shipment safety; duplicate action replay; assignment contention; completed history.
010716 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:15 records: Consignment scenarios: Pick to Pack; Pick to Mark to Pack; missing optional default becomes Pick to Pack; assembly routes remain blocked; assignment, view-only, duplicate-action, and cross-account rules; Flipkart and Amazon imports.
010717 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:17 records: Scanner scenarios: AWB, tracking ID, seller SKU, FSN, listing ID, ASIN, FNSKU, barcode, task ID, consignment number, identifiers duplicated across accounts, completed-only result, and no result.
010718 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:19 records: Permission profiles: owner/manager administration; picker pick-only; marker mark-only; assembler assemble-only; packer pack-only; view-all read access without mutation; explicit account assignment; cross-account denial; problem-report capability; library/rule/consignment permissions.
010719 | Evidence catalogue docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md:21 records: Concurrency reporting must remain precise: request levels 2/5/10/20 stress duplicate quantity increments for one mutation and one action log. Other action families retain targeted or two-request contention coverage. Do not generalize the 20-request result to every action.

010724 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:1 records: # Marking Library
010725 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:3 records: The Marking Library is an owner-managed design and process foundation. It is not inventory management and does not track physical stock, valuation, receiving, QC, reservations, or marketplace quantities.
010726 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:5 records: ## Assets And Files
010727 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:7 records: `MarkingAsset` stores design metadata. `MarkingAssetFile` stores immutable version metadata while the binary remains under `storage/marking-library/`. Replacement uploads create a new version and deactivate the previous version; history and the owner original remain available. ZIP files are stored without extraction.
010728 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:9 records: Back up the database and `storage/marking-library/` together. A database backup alone cannot restore marking files.
010729 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:11 records: Allowed marking formats are deliberately conservative: EZD, DXF, SVG, AI, CDR, PLT/HPGL, G-code/NC, PDF, and stored ZIP. Preview images allow PNG/JPEG/WebP. Reports allow PDF/TXT/CSV. Executables and scripts are blocked. The referenced EngravingBrain repository was not available in this workspace, so no dependency or unverified proprietary format behavior was copied.
010730 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:13 records: ## Listing Links And Matching
010731 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:15 records: One design may be explicitly linked to many marketplace listings and accounts. Every link records its account, marketplace, match method, actor, and safe identifier snapshot. Exact matching never crosses the selected account.
010732 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:17 records: Priority is seller SKU, marketplace product identifiers, barcodes, model number, then internal/external identifiers. A unique exact result may be proposed; multiple exact results require owner selection. Titles are only a manual fallback.
010733 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:19 records: ## Process Routes
010734 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:21 records: - `PICK_PACK`: ready-made product.
010735 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:22 records: - `PICK_MARK_PACK`: linked marking asset required.
010736 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:23 records: - `PICK_ASSEMBLE_PACK`: assembly configuration required.
010737 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:24 records: - `PICK_MARK_ASSEMBLE_PACK`: marking asset and assembly configuration required.
010738 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:26 records: Changing a rule does not alter existing order state in Phase 1. Current picker and packer flows continue using `Order.pickStatus` and `Order.packStatus`.
010739 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:28 records: ## Permissions
010740 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:30 records: Owners have all capabilities through server-side bypass. Workers may independently receive Pick, Mark, Assemble, Pack, Report Problem, Manage Marking Library, Manage Process Rules, and View All Work permissions. Assigned accounts continue to restrict worker data.
010741 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:32 records: ## Metadata Validation
010742 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_LIBRARY.md:34 records: Asset status is limited to `DRAFT`, `ACTIVE`, or `ARCHIVED`. Names cannot be blank; settings must be valid JSON; passes must be a positive whole number up to 1000; dimensions are non-negative; power is capped at 100, speed at 100000, and frequency at 1000. Optional machine values may remain unset.
010743 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKER_FILE_DELIVERY.md:1 records: # Future Marking Worker File Delivery
010744 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKER_FILE_DELIVERY.md:3 records: This contract is documentation only. No Worker Agent or automatic file delivery is active in Phase 1.
010745 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKER_FILE_DELIVERY.md:5 records: The owner server keeps the original active marking file permanently. A future authenticated task endpoint may issue a short-lived download, return the expected SHA-256, and record download acknowledgement. The endpoint must authorize stage permission and assigned account every time.

010750 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKER_FILE_DELIVERY.md:17 records: A later Windows agent may download into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\MarketplaceWorker\jobs\<taskId>\`, verify SHA-256, open through Windows association or a reviewed `engravingbrain://` protocol, report `OPENED`, wait for worker completion, delete only its temporary local copy, retry locked-file deletion, and clean stale jobs.
010751 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKER_FILE_DELIVERY.md:19 records: The owner original is never deleted. Browser-only download remains available, and the server never assumes a browser can silently delete a local file.
010752 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKFLOW.md:1 records: # Marking Workflow
010753 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKFLOW.md:3 records: `/work/marking` is available to active users with `canMark` in the selected account. Cards show immutable Flipkart or Amazon product identity, image, route, quantity progress, Master Design ID, instructions, machine settings, assignment, and problem state. Full catalog details and up to ten images load on `/work/marking/[taskId]`.
010754 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKFLOW.md:5 records: Worker cards do not offer marking-file open/download controls in Phase 6. The owner marking library, stored versions, and secure task-scoped route remain preserved for future use; managed paths are never returned.
010755 | Evidence catalogue docs/MARKING_WORKFLOW.md:7 records: The owner copy remains private. EngravingBrain, Worker Agent delivery, temporary files, hash verification, and cleanup automation remain later optional work.
010756 | Evidence catalogue docs/OPTIONAL_DEFAULT_PROCESSING.md:1 records: # Optional Default Processing
010757 | Evidence catalogue docs/OPTIONAL_DEFAULT_PROCESSING.md:3 records: `ProductProcessRule` is a convenience default, not a validity requirement.
010758 | Evidence catalogue docs/OPTIONAL_DEFAULT_PROCESSING.md:5 records: - No active applicable rule: Pick -> Pack.
010759 | Evidence catalogue docs/OPTIONAL_DEFAULT_PROCESSING.md:6 records: - `PICK_PACK`: Direct to Pack.
010760 | Evidence catalogue docs/OPTIONAL_DEFAULT_PROCESSING.md:7 records: - `PICK_MARK_PACK`: Marking.

010764 | Evidence catalogue docs/ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PACKING_GATE.md:1 records: # Order Assembly Packing Gate
010765 | Evidence catalogue docs/ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PACKING_GATE.md:3 records: `packCustomerOrderShipmentSafely()` applies the gate inside its final transaction after account authorization and shipment scope resolution.
010766 | Evidence catalogue docs/ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PACKING_GATE.md:5 records: Packing is allowed when no assembly is required, or the order's Assembly task is `COMPLETED` or `SKIPPED`. It is blocked for required tasks that are missing, `LOCKED`, `READY`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `PROBLEM`, or `CANCELLED`, and for ambiguous/invalid/unsupported customer-order rules.
010767 | Evidence catalogue docs/ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PACKING_GATE.md:7 records: For Flipkart, the gate checks every active row sharing `accountId + marketplace + Tracking ID`. One blocked sibling prevents every row from packing; no partial shipment update occurs. Already-packed siblings remain outside the active scope.
010768 | Evidence catalogue docs/ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PACKING_GATE.md:9 records: The browser, universal scanner, direct Pack card, packing detail, and mobile packing endpoint all converge on this shared packing service.
010769 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:1 records: # Performance Benchmarks
010770 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:3 records: Results are machine-specific observations, not guaranteed production latency.
010771 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:5 records: ## Universal Resolver
010772 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:7 records: Small preset database: 2 accounts, 5,000 listings/identifiers, 1,000 tasks, 500 orders, 6,381,568-byte SQLite file.
010773 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:9 records: - Warm p50 across scenarios: about 66-152 ms in the final full-suite run.
010774 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:10 records: - Warm p95 across scenarios: about 81-389 ms.
010775 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:11 records: - Warm maximum observed: 389.17 ms.
010776 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:12 records: - Cold observed range: about 57-186 ms.
010777 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:13 records: - Assigned-task case remained present; candidate limit remained enforced.
010778 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:14 records: - Heap observation: about 16.1 MB before and 33.5 MB after the final measured run.
010779 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:16 records: Run larger local presets without committing databases:
010780 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:18 records: ```powershell
010781 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:19 records: npm.cmd run phase7:generate-scale -- medium
010782 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:20 records: npx.cmd tsx scripts/full-universal-resolver-benchmark.ts medium
010783 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:23 records: The 800,000-listing preset is available but was not run in this pass because this owner PC is a 2-core/4-thread i3 and the full generation is intentionally expensive.
010784 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:25 records: ## Import Parsing
010785 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:27 records: - Flipkart fake listing rows: 30,000 parsed in 6,934 ms; planning 357 ms; 256 MB RSS.
010786 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:28 records: - Flipkart fake order rows: 1,000 parsed in 132 ms; planning 79 ms.
010787 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:29 records: - Amazon fake shipment rows: 10,000 parsed in approximately 8.4-12.1 seconds across focused and full-suite runs.
010788 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERFORMANCE_BENCHMARKS.md:31 records: These parser measurements exclude database matching, browser transport, and activation. Preview remains non-activating; activation remains explicit and transactional.
010789 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERMISSION_MATRIX.md:1 records: # Permission Matrix

010802 | Evidence catalogue docs/PERMISSION_MATRIX.md:16 records: Every mutation rechecks active user, active/assigned account, stage permission, source identity, expected state, and request replay. Client account IDs are selectors, never authorization proof. Removing account access invalidates subsequent actions.
010803 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 1 Marking And Workflow Foundation Plan
010804 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:3 records: ## Current Architecture
010805 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:5 records: Marketplace Pick & Pack is a Next.js 15 application with Prisma 6, SQLite for the owner-PC deployment, and a parallel PostgreSQL schema/migration tree. Accounts scope listings, imports, orders, scans, problems, and worker assignments. Existing live work uses `Order.pickStatus` and `Order.packStatus`; those fields remain authoritative.
010806 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:7 records: Authentication uses signed HTTP-only sessions backed by `UserDeviceSession`. Owners can access all active accounts, while workers are limited to assigned active accounts. Audit events are written through the existing `AuditLog` service. Listing imports create or update `MarketplaceListing` records in account-scoped batches.
010807 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:9 records: ## Additive Migration Strategy
010808 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:11 records: The migration adds five `User` permission columns with safe `false` defaults, identifier/marking/process/work-task tables, foreign keys, uniqueness constraints, and indexed exact-match paths. Existing users, accounts, listings, orders, imports, scans, and pick/pack statuses are not rewritten. Existing listings are backfilled into the identifier registry idempotently. No `WorkTask` rows are created for existing orders.
010809 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:13 records: Both `prisma/schema.prisma` and `prisma/schema.postgres.prisma` receive equivalent models. SQLite and PostgreSQL migrations are maintained separately using their existing repository conventions.
010810 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:15 records: ## Models Added
010811 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:17 records: - `MarketplaceListingIdentifier`: account-aware exact identifiers with raw and normalized values.
010812 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:18 records: - `MarkingAsset`: owner-managed design metadata independent of any engraving database.
010813 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:19 records: - `MarkingAssetFile`: immutable managed file versions; binary data stays on disk.
010814 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:20 records: - `MarkingAssetListingLink`: explicit many-listing and multi-account links.
010815 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:21 records: - `ProductProcessRule`: one service-enforced active route per marketplace listing.
010816 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:22 records: - `WorkTask`: dormant generic stage/task foundation for later order and consignment workflows.
010817 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:24 records: ## Storage Design
010818 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:26 records: Files live below `storage/marking-library/<assetId>/<fileId>/` under randomized managed names. SQLite stores only relative managed paths and metadata. Central helpers enforce size/count limits, extension allowlists, executable blocklists, path containment, zero-byte rejection, SHA-256 calculation, duplicate detection, and cleanup of partial writes. ZIP files are stored but never extracted in this phase. Downloads are authenticated and never expose absolute paths.
010819 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:28 records: ## Authorization Design
010820 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:30 records: `OWNER` receives every new capability through server-side bypass logic even when legacy owner rows contain default `false` values. Workers may receive stage and management permissions independently, but account assignment still limits every listing, link, rule, file, and future task operation. Management actions re-check authorization server-side and never trust a client-supplied account ID as proof.
010821 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:32 records: ## Listing Matching
010822 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:34 records: Identifier matching is selected-account scoped and exact. Seller SKU is checked first, then marketplace product identifiers, barcode identifiers, and model number. Results are `EXACT_UNIQUE`, `EXACT_MULTIPLE`, `NOT_FOUND`, or `INVALID`. Ambiguous results are never auto-selected. Product title remains a manual search aid only.
010823 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:36 records: ## Non-Goals
010824 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:38 records: - No inventory balance, ledger, receiving, QC, valuation, reservation, or marketplace stock update.
010825 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:39 records: - No activated `WorkTask` integration with current orders.
010826 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:40 records: - No consignment worker flow.
010827 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:41 records: - No universal scan page.
010828 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:42 records: - No Windows Worker Agent or automatic deletion of owner files.
010829 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:43 records: - No Android/mobile-app changes.
010830 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:45 records: ## Rollback Concerns
010831 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_1_MARKING_FOUNDATION_PLAN.md:47 records: Application rollback is safe while new tables are unused by current pick/pack flows. Database rollback should preserve marking files and metadata; dropping the new tables is intentionally not automated. Back up the database and `storage/marking-library/` together. The owner original marking file is never deleted by normal workflow completion.

010839 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 2: Flipkart Consignment Import and Activation
010840 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:5 records: Phase 1 provides a generic listing identifier registry, marking assets and immutable managed files, per-listing process rules, dormant `WorkTask` records, and granular worker permissions. Existing customer Order Pick/Pack continues to use its original status fields and routes.
010841 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:7 records: ## Phase 1 issues addressed here

010846 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:14 records: ## Additive migration
010847 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:16 records: Phase 2 adds consignment enums, batches, lines, supporting-file metadata, issues, three user permissions, and relations. Only the dormant `WorkTask` table may be rebuilt on SQLite to add CHECK constraints and foreign keys. The existing `Order` table and order statuses are not changed.
010848 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:18 records: ## Import architecture
010849 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:20 records: CSV and ZIP uploads are size-checked before buffering. ZIP entries are enumerated lazily, bounded by entry count/name length/extracted bytes, checked for traversal and unsafe types, and classified by decoded headers/content. Exactly one Consignment Details CSV is required. Supporting label, QC-reference, README, and unknown safe files create metadata only.
010850 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:22 records: The parser normalizes headers and rows, treats `Quantity Sent` strictly as required work quantity, and records safe structured issues. Identifier matching is batched and account-scoped: Seller SKU first, then FSN. Conflicts and ambiguity always require owner resolution; titles are never auto-matched.
010851 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:24 records: ## Preview architecture
010852 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:26 records: `/owner/consignments` lists batches. New uploads are created at `/owner/consignments/new`. Detail, review, and issue routes use paginated database queries (50 rows by default), compact listing projections, lazy product images, summary counts, and account-scoped actions. No tasks exist during preview.
010853 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:28 records: ## Activation transaction
010854 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:30 records: Activation reclaims the batch with a guarded status update, re-reads all lines, validates listing/route/marking requirements, writes immutable snapshots, creates stage plans in chunks, marks lines activated, and moves the batch to ACTIVE in one transaction. A second or concurrent activation cannot create duplicate tasks. Audit metadata contains identifiers and counts only.
010855 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:32 records: ## Task integrity
010856 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:34 records: - ORDER tasks require only `orderId`; CONSIGNMENT tasks require only `consignmentLineId`.
010857 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:35 records: - Account ownership is checked in services.
010858 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:36 records: - Required quantity and sequence are positive integers; completed quantity is bounded.
010859 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:37 records: - A source cannot have duplicate stages or sequence numbers.
010860 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:38 records: - First stage is READY and later stages are LOCKED.
010861 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:39 records: - Transition helpers use guarded database updates and never mutate merely because a code was scanned.
010862 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:41 records: ## Permissions
010863 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:43 records: `canViewConsignments`, `canImportConsignments`, and `canManageConsignments` are account-scoped. OWNER bypasses all three. Import permits draft upload/parsing; management permits mapping, route resolution, cancellation, and activation.
010864 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:47 records: Tests cover parser aliases and invalid quantities, ZIP classification and traversal rejection, account-scoped matching/conflicts, SQLite CHECK and unique constraints, activation idempotency, route task plans, snapshots, supporting-file non-activation, permission boundaries, and regression checks for old Order Pick/Pack.
010865 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:49 records: ## Explicit non-goals
010866 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_2_FLIPKART_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:51 records: No inventory, stock ledger, receiving, QC workflow, label printing, destination stock, worker consignment pages, mobile API, or APK changes are part of Phase 2.
010867 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:1 records: # Phase 3.1 Workflow Hardening
010868 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:5 records: Phase 3 incorrectly combined `canViewAllWork` with manager controls and backend problem resolution. Idempotency was also checked before authorization and identified requests only by task plus request ID. Completed history had no day boundary, and Work Hub counts were account-wide for ordinary workers.
010869 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:7 records: ## Permission Matrix
010870 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:9 records: - `canViewAllWork`: read all applicable selected-account work and problems. It does not grant progress, resolution, assignment, or stage permission.
010871 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:10 records: - `canManageConsignments`: view all selected-account consignment work, resolve problems, and assign or reassign tasks. It does not bypass selected-account access.
010872 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:11 records: - `OWNER`: existing full active-account policy.
010873 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:12 records: - Normal worker: sees unassigned or own stage work and own assigned/reported problems; mutation still requires stage and assignment permission.
010874 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:14 records: The shared helpers are `userCanViewAllConsignmentWork`, `userCanManageConsignmentTasks`, `userCanResolveConsignmentProblems`, and `getWorkTaskCapabilities`.
010875 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:16 records: ## Idempotency
010876 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:18 records: Every replay-capable service authorizes the active actor, selected account, task or batch, stage/action permission, and assignment before checking a prior request. `WorkActionLog.requestKind` distinguishes claim, increment, set, complete, problem report/resolution, and assignment families. Replay also verifies actor and expected action type.
010877 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:20 records: The database preserves existing rows, backfills request kinds, and uses a unique task/actor/request-kind/request-ID key plus a task/request-ID lookup index. The latter detects cross-actor and cross-operation collisions. Concurrent progress retries reauthorize, reread the committed log, and return the stored result; unrelated database errors remain errors.
010878 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:22 records: ## Queue Semantics
010879 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:24 records: Completed history defaults to `completedAt` on or after the local application-day boundary. Normal-worker hub counts include unassigned or own READY work, only own IN_PROGRESS work, own assigned/reported problems, and work completed by that worker today. Owner, manager, and view-all counts may be account-wide.
010880 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:26 records: ## Migration Decision
010881 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:28 records: SQLite uses one additive migration. PostgreSQL creates the enum and column first, then backfills and indexes in a follow-up migration so a newly added enum value is not consumed in the same migration transaction. No task or action history is deleted.
010882 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:30 records: ## Test Strategy
010883 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_1_WORKFLOW_HARDENING.md:32 records: Temporary migrated SQLite tests cover view-only denial, manager resolution, assignment denial, actor/action request collisions, concurrent duplicate increments, day filtering, and worker/account-wide count differences. Source-policy checks ensure UI controls consume centralized capabilities. Existing parser, marking, consignment, mobile-source, migration, and build regressions remain mandatory.
010884 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 3: Consignment Worker Workflow Plan
010885 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:3 records: ## Current architecture
010886 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:5 records: Phase 2 stores account-scoped Flipkart consignment batches, lines, supporting files, issues, immutable activation snapshots, and dormant stage plans. Activation creates READY/LOCKED WorkTask rows transactionally without modifying customer Order statuses.
010887 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:7 records: ## Worker routes
010888 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:9 records: - /work: permission-aware Work Hub.
010889 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:10 records: - /work/consignments/pick: active consignment PICK queue and exact scanner/manual lookup.
010890 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:11 records: - /work/marking: active MARK queue, marking settings, preview and private file access.
010891 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:12 records: - /work/consignments/pack: active PACK queue and exact lookup.
010892 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:13 records: - /work/problems: task-level problem queue and manager resolution.
010893 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:14 records: - /work/tasks/[taskId]/marking-file: authenticated task-scoped stream.
010894 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:16 records: ## Authorization
010895 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:18 records: Every mutation re-reads the active user, selected account, assigned active accounts, stage permission, task account, line account, and assignment. OWNER has active-account bypass. Form account IDs are selectors only. Viewing all work does not by itself grant stage mutation.
010896 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:20 records: ## Assignment
010897 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:22 records: Unassigned READY work is visible to permitted workers. The first mutation atomically claims it. Assigned work is mutable only by the assignee or OWNER. Managers may assign, reassign, or unassign only to active account-assigned users with the required stage permission. The next stage is unlocked unassigned.
010898 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:24 records: ## Progress transactions
010899 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:26 records: Progress accepts whole nondecreasing quantities, expected current quantity, and optional clientRequestId. A unique task/request log makes retries idempotent. Guarded updates reject stale pages, LOCKED/PROBLEM/CANCELLED work, over-completion, and simultaneous conflicting claims. Scan/search alone never mutates.
010900 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:28 records: ## Completion
010901 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:30 records: Completing one stage unlocks exactly the next sequence. Final PACK completion timestamps the line. Central reconciliation keeps a progressing batch ACTIVE, sets PROBLEM while any task is problematic, restores ACTIVE after resolution, and marks COMPLETED only when all final PACK tasks complete.
010902 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:32 records: ## Marking file access
010903 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:34 records: A marker with selected-account and assignment access may stream only the active MARKING_FILE linked to that MARK task. Node streams are converted with Readable.toWeb, responses are private/no-store/nosniff, paths are never disclosed, and every download is recorded in WorkActionLog.
010904 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:38 records: Reporting preserves quantity and assignment, records category/note/actor/time, blocks later work, and moves batch reconciliation to PROBLEM. Manager resolution preserves history and returns zero-progress work to READY or partial work to IN_PROGRESS.
010905 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:40 records: ## Performance
010906 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:42 records: Queues use indexed account/source/stage/status/assignment filters, compact includes, cursor-like pagination at 50, lazy images, and separate counts. Activation precomputes snapshots and uses chunked createMany plus batched raw snapshot updates while retaining one atomic transaction. Benchmarks cover 100, 1,000, and 10,000 lines.
010907 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:44 records: Local temporary-SQLite benchmark (`npm.cmd run workflow:benchmark`): 100 lines / 200 tasks in 186 ms; 1,000 lines / 2,000 tasks in 1,155 ms; 10,000 lines / 20,000 tasks in 11,099 ms. Timings vary by machine, but every run verifies the exact persisted task count.
010908 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:48 records: Worker cards prioritize image, SKU, required/completed/remaining quantity, assignment, and one obvious action. Mobile uses Work/Pick/Pack/Problems/Account navigation, 44px controls, no mandatory tables, and content padding above the bottom bar.
010909 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:50 records: ## Non-goals
010910 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:52 records: No inventory, receiving, QC, stock accounting, customer-order Assembly, universal cross-account scanner, automatic marking-agent deletion, mobile API changes, or APK work.
010911 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKER_PLAN.md:56 records: Temporary migrated SQLite tests cover authorization, claiming races, idempotent progress, stage unlocking, completion, problems, exact search, streamed marking files, assignment rules, migration constraints, activation scale, and all existing Order/Meesho/Flipkart regressions.
010912 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:1 records: # Phase 4.1 Universal Scanner Corrections
010913 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:3 records: Base commit: `02879e8302804eb805f985eb3e70817763982043`
010914 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:5 records: ## Shipment packing safety
010915 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:7 records: Universal Flipkart packing now resolves the active shipment from the primary order on the server. A Flipkart shipment is grouped by account, marketplace, and Tracking ID. Every non-packed sibling must be picked, ready to pack, and free of problem status before any row is changed.
010916 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:9 records: The final transaction repeats account authorization, re-reads the primary order and complete active shipment, validates every row, updates only the verified IDs, writes one scan log per changed row, and writes one masked shipment audit summary. The browser never supplies a trusted sibling-order list.
010917 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:11 records: Blocked messages are explicit:
010918 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:13 records: - `Shipment cannot be packed: X item(s) are still waiting for picking.`
010919 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:14 records: - `Shipment contains problem work.`
010920 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:16 records: Already packed rows are excluded from active scope and remain unchanged. Repeated or concurrent attempts are idempotent at the order state boundary.
010921 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:18 records: ## Scanner candidates
010922 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:20 records: One active Flipkart Tracking ID produces one shipment-level Pack candidate. The card shows its Tracking ID, representative AWB values, order and shipment references, item count, total quantity, picked count, waiting count, and product summary. Order-level Pick candidates remain separate and show their own AWB, Tracking ID, order number, shipment ID, and SKU.
010923 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:22 records: Consignment cards show the consignment number, stage, short task reference, source row, Seller SKU, FSN, Listing ID, and required/done/remaining quantities. Mark tasks also show Master Design ID, asset name, position, dimensions, power, speed, frequency, passes, instructions, and protected preview/download links. Managed storage paths are never returned.
010924 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:24 records: Identifier match reasons use this deterministic priority:
010925 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:27 records: 2. Seller SKU
010926 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:30 records: 5. Listing ID
010927 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:36 records: 11. Internal SKU
010928 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:37 records: 12. External ID
010929 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:39 records: Assigned exact tasks are queried before general exact matches, merged, deduplicated, ranked, and only then bounded. This prevents a worker's assigned task from disappearing behind many unassigned matches.
010930 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:41 records: ## Next-scan behavior
010931 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:43 records: Lookup retains the submitted code while candidates are reviewed. A successful action redirects without `q`, so the input is empty and focused for the next scan. A failed or stale action preserves the code, selects all text, and focuses the field so the next hardware scan replaces it. Enter remains lookup-only; scanning never performs a mutation automatically.
010932 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:45 records: ## Permissions and problem visibility
010933 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:47 records: The Universal Scanner is available to owners and users with Pick, Mark, Pack, View All Work, or Manage Consignments permission. Users without scanner-related permission are redirected.
010934 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:49 records: Customer packing counts, latest batch data, old-pending controls, and the legacy AWB panel are queried and rendered only for owners or Pack-authorized users.
010935 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:51 records: Customer-order problem candidates are read-only. They are visible to owners, Pack-authorized users, View All Work users, and the worker who reported that specific problem. `canReportProblem` by itself does not reveal every customer problem in an authorized account.
010936 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_1_SCANNER_CORRECTIONS.md:53 records: ## Manual QA

010949 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 4 Universal Scanner Plan
010950 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:5 records: The scanner resolves exact active work across every account the current user is authorized to use. Owners receive all active accounts. Workers receive active assigned accounts plus their active legacy primary account, deduplicated by ID. An optional account filter must be within that set.
010951 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:7 records: The resolver searches compact customer-order fields and listing identifiers, then loads bounded active consignment tasks. It returns separate cards for every matching source, account, and stage. Scanning is lookup-only; mutations require an explicit card action and a second server-side authorization check.
010952 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:9 records: ## Matching And Results
010953 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:11 records: Priority is AWB/Tracking ID, FNSKU, Seller SKU, FSN/ASIN, listing identifiers, barcode identifiers, order identifiers, then an explicit work-task ID. Scan mode never uses title or fuzzy matching. Actionable cards sort first, followed by the current worker's assignments, match priority, ready state, and a stable candidate key.
010954 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:13 records: Packed orders and completed tasks are excluded from active cards and contribute only to `completedMatchCount`. Visible problems are read-only scanner candidates. Multiple matches are never auto-selected.
010955 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:15 records: ## Security And Actions
010956 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:17 records: The account ID from a form is a selector, never authorization proof. Every action recalculates account access, reloads the source with its account predicate, checks permission, assignment, expected state or quantity, and then calls the existing order/task domain behavior. The selected-account session is not changed. Result payloads omit raw rows, secrets, private paths, and database configuration.
010957 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:19 records: ## Performance And Indexes
010958 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:21 records: Universal code-first indexes cover order operational identifiers and listing `identifierType + normalizedValue + accountId`. Queries are exact and bounded to at most 50 returned candidates. The integration test uses the real resolver; the separate SQLite microbenchmark validates the 800,000-identifier index path with `EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN`.
010959 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:23 records: ## UI And Tests
010960 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:25 records: `/work/scan` and `/packing` reuse `UniversalScannerPanel`. The existing customer-order scanner remains available under Customer Order Packing. The UI uses a focused input, intent/account filters, prominent account/marketplace labels, compact cards, explicit actions, and completed-only/no-result states.
010961 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:27 records: Tests cover account scope, exact identifiers, duplicate-account matches, order and consignment candidates, problem visibility, completed exclusion, deterministic ordering, bounded results, explicit actions, stale authorization, idempotency, indexes, and the Phase 3.1 regressions.
010962 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:29 records: ## Non-goals
010963 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_4_UNIVERSAL_SCANNER_PLAN.md:31 records: No mobile-app changes, APK work, inventory, ERP behavior, customer-order Assembly, Amazon consignment import, fuzzy title search, AI Product Design Identity, or Worker Agent is included.
010964 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 5 Customer Order Assembly Plan
010965 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:3 records: ## Lifecycle
010966 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:5 records: - Ready-made product: `PICK -> PACK -> COMPLETED`.
010967 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:6 records: - Assembly rule: `PICK -> ASSEMBLE -> PACK -> COMPLETED`.
010968 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:7 records: - Manual exception: a pack-authorized worker sends a picked order to Assembly, then normal packing resumes after completion.
010969 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:9 records: One `WorkTask` with source `ORDER` and stage `ASSEMBLE` represents the work. `Order.pickStatus` and `Order.packStatus` remain authoritative. The task's immutable `metadataJson` snapshot contains versioned instructions, safe image URLs, SKU/title snapshots, rule identity, requester, and request time.
010970 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:11 records: ## Rule Matching
010971 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:13 records: Automatic matching is account-scoped and exact. Seller SKU is checked first, then internal SKU through `MarketplaceListingIdentifier`. Titles are never matched. Ambiguous listings, invalid rules, and `PICK_MARK_ASSEMBLE_PACK` customer-order routes require owner review instead of guessing.
010972 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:15 records: ## Worker Flow
010973 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:17 records: - A successful Pick creates the task automatically for `PICK_ASSEMBLE_PACK`.
010974 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:18 records: - A pack-authorized worker can manually send a picked, unpacked, non-problem order with instructions.
010975 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:19 records: - An assembly worker claims, completes, or reports a problem on a task.
010976 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:20 records: - Only an owner can reassign, resolve a problem, or skip assembly with a reason.
010977 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:21 records: - The packing transaction rechecks all active shipment rows. Any pending/problem/invalid assembly state blocks the entire shipment.
010978 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:23 records: ## Permissions
010979 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:25 records: - Pick: owner or `canPick` (legacy Picker remains compatible).
010980 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:26 records: - Send to Assembly: owner or `canPack`.
010981 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:27 records: - Assembly progress: owner or `canAssemble`, with account and assignment checks.
010982 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:28 records: - View all assembly work: owner or `canViewAllWork` (read-only without `canAssemble`).
010983 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:29 records: - Resolve, reassign, skip: owner only in Phase 5.
010984 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:31 records: ## Non-goals
010985 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_5_ORDER_ASSEMBLY_PLAN.md:33 records: No BOM, parts inventory, stock deduction, multi-step manufacturing, assembly stations, costing, QC, ERP, Worker Agent, Amazon consignment, APK, or mobile-app changes are included.

010988 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_1_AMAZON_CORRECTIONS.md:5 records: No mobile, Expo, Android, EngravingBrain, Worker Agent, inventory, or ERP work is included.
010989 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_1_AMAZON_CORRECTIONS.md:7 records: Catalog authority is deterministic by safe file/table/row provenance. All Listings controls identity/status; catalog reports enrich product display fields. Blank cells never erase nonblank values, and conflicting nonblank catalog values create `CATALOG_FIELD_CONFLICT` warnings without storing raw rows or managed paths.

010991 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_2_CATALOG_SNAPSHOT_ENRICHMENT.md:3 records: Amazon tables now carry explicit sheet usage and priority. Only operational or unknown data sheets may participate in identity, enrichment, or shipment quantities; reference sheets remain stored but inert.
010992 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_2_CATALOG_SNAPSHOT_ENRICHMENT.md:5 records: Catalog authority produces `AmazonListingEnrichmentV1` values keyed by listing ID. 010994 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:1 records: # Phase 6 Amazon Consignment Plan
010995 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:3 records: Amazon extends the existing generic Flipkart consignment architecture. It uses the same `ConsignmentBatch`, `ConsignmentLine`, owner review, transactional activation, `WorkTask` stages, assignments, problems, universal scanner, and line/batch reconciliation.
010996 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:5 records: ## Sources And Classification
010997 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:7 records: Supported files are CSV, TSV/TXT, XLSX, XLSM, and bounded ZIP archives. Header signatures classify shipment, All Listings, category catalog, product catalog, and supporting data. Filenames are not authoritative. XLSM cells are read as workbook data only; macros and formulas are never executed.
010998 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:9 records: Shipment rows retain bounded operational fields only. All Listings is authoritative for Seller SKU, ASIN, FNSKU, and listing status. Catalog files enrich nonblank title, category, brand, descriptions, bullets, attributes, and up to ten safe image URLs. Raw rows and private paths are not retained.
010999 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:11 records: ## Matching And Activation
011000 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:13 records: Exact matching is scoped to the selected Amazon account. Priority is FNSKU, Seller SKU, ASIN, External ID, then EAN/UPC/GTIN. Conflicting or duplicate identifiers require owner review; titles never auto-match. Upload and preview create no tasks.
011001 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:15 records: Activation supports `PICK_PACK` and `PICK_MARK_PACK`. It copies immutable Amazon identifiers and a versioned catalog snapshot to the line, then creates the existing stage plan. Marking requires an active asset and meaningful instructions or Master Design ID. A worker marking file is not required.
011002 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:17 records: ## Security And Performance
011003 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:19 records: ZIP paths, symlinks, encrypted entries, nested archives, executables, entry counts, depth, and expanded bytes are bounded. Workbooks have size, sheet, row, column, cell-length, and total-cell limits. Matching and identifier writes are batched, queue queries remain bounded, and snapshots contain no raw spreadsheet rows.
011004 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:21 records: ## Migration And Tests
011005 | Evidence catalogue docs/PHASE_6_AMAZON_CONSIGNMENT_PLAN.md:23 records: SQLite uses a safe-copy migration to widen the existing Flipkart-only batch constraint while preserving all rows and indexes. PostgreSQL receives additive enum values, columns, and indexes. Tests cover classification, workbook and ZIP safety, exact scoped matching, preview, enrichment, activation, immutable snapshots, migration compatibility, scanner behavior, and a synthetic 10,000-row parse.

011060 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:1 records: # Marketplace Pick & Pack: project master guide
011061 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:3 records: ## 1–5. Purpose, non-goals, structure, deployment, and SQLite
011062 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:5 records: The application coordinates marketplace catalog imports, marking references, customer-order and consignment work, scanning, assignment, problems, and pack completion for a small owner-operated warehouse. It is a Next.js application with UI/routes under `app`, reusable UI under `components`, domain services under `src/lib`, Prisma schemas/migrations under `prisma`, tests under `tests`, operational scripts under `scripts`, and decisions/runbooks under `docs`.
011063 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:7 records: The local owner-PC deployment uses a private SQLite database and private filesystem storage. A parallel PostgreSQL schema/migration tree exists for a future production deployment. Product Inventory is a marketplace listing catalog; physical inventory, stock balances, purchasing, accounting, ERP ledgers, and automatic marketplace synchronization are explicit non-goals.
011064 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:9 records: ## 6–8. Authentication, permissions, and account assignment
011065 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:11 records: Users authenticate with username and a stored password hash. Roles provide broad identity while explicit capability flags control Pick, Mark, Assembly, Pack, problem reporting, marking-library management, process-rule management, view-all work, and consignment operations. Active state, forced password change, login-failure lockout, sessions, password-reset requests, and audit logs support operational security.
011066 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:13 records: Seller accounts scope listings, identifiers, orders, consignments, tasks, actions, scans, and imports. A user may have a selected account and assigned-account relation. Server actions must authorize capability and account; hiding a UI control is not authorization.
011067 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:15 records: ## 9–12. Catalog, identifiers, defaults, and marking
011068 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:17 records: MarketplaceListing is the canonical per-account catalog row. MarketplaceListingIdentifier stores exact lookup values such as SKU, FSN, listing ID, ASIN, FNSKU, EAN/UPC/GTIN, model, barcode, and external ID. Imports merge through marketplace-specific classifiers and services; account scoping prevents ambiguous cross-account mutation.
011069 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:19 records: ProductProcessRule is optional. Missing rules default to Pick to Pack. MarkingAsset, files, and listing links provide reusable warehouse marking material; database and marking-library files must be backed up together.
011070 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:21 records: ## 13–17. Customer, assembly, consignment, Flipkart, and Amazon workflows
011071 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:23 records: Customer orders retain their established pick/pack fields while WorkTask represents staged work. Supported routes include Pick-Pack, Pick-Mark-Pack, Pick-Assembly-Pack, and Pick-Mark-Assembly-Pack for customer work where enabled. Task sequence, locks, assignments, quantities, problems, completions, and immutable action logs enforce safe progression.
011072 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:25 records: Consignments import shipment lines, resolve exact catalog matches, snapshot identifiers/content, review issues, select optional processing, and activate tasks. Flipkart and Amazon parsers have marketplace-specific evidence and safety policies. Amazon reference worksheets are excluded by default and submitted worksheet selections are revalidated server-side. Consignment assembly remains intentionally blocked.
011073 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:27 records: ## 18–22. Scanner, packing, problems, imports, and images
011074 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:29 records: The universal scanner resolves exact work across order, tracking, identifiers, tasks, consignments, snapshots, and completed results within authorized accounts. Packing checks shipment-wide readiness, including multi-item Flipkart safety. Problem reporting pauses work; authorized resolution restores controlled state. Assignment and view-all permissions are distinct.
011075 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:31 records: ImportJob records background import progress, reports, cancellation, and recovery data. Upload batches retain preview/issue information. Private source files live in ignored storage. Image mappings and cache health accelerate worker cards without turning the catalog into stock inventory.
011076 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:33 records: ## 23–28. Backup, reset, security, idempotency, concurrency, performance
011077 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:35 records: Real-database migrations use inspect, verified backup, copied-database deployment tests, unchanged-source proof, explicit typed confirmation, and post-migration verification. The fresh-start workflow adds exact owner selection, complete dynamic table inventory, database/storage backup, disposable reset proof, three typed confirmations, and a manual restore helper.
011078 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:37 records: Security depends on server-side authentication, account/capability authorization, bounded upload/archive/parser limits, safe file paths, input validation, and private ignored operational data. Client request IDs and task/action uniqueness make replay idempotent. Quantity progress uses bounded retry and safe replay recovery; some older action paths retain narrower transaction-based replay behavior and must not be described as universally transaction-free.
011079 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:39 records: The measured resolver result is for the small preset: 2 accounts, 5,000 listings, 1,000 tasks, and 500 orders. Representative exact query plans cover AWB, tracking, identifiers, queue, assignment, and snapshot FNSKU. Medium-or-larger testing on stronger hardware remains a production gate.
011080 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:41 records: ## 29–33. Responsive QA, limitations, manual QA, native plan, APK plan
011081 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:43 records: Responsive manual checks are mandatory at 360, 390, 430, 768, 1024, and 1440 pixels. Warehouse tests must use sanitized fixtures and cover every stage, problems, assignments, contention, files, and scanner hardware. Automated approval does not approve production rollout.
011082 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:45 records: Current limitations include unproven 800,000-listing performance, representative rather than exhaustive query-plan proof, high-concurrency 2/5/10/20 coverage specific to duplicate quantity increments, and older replay callbacks in some action families. Phase 7.2B is not implemented on this checkpoint branch, and Phase 7.2C is not implemented.
011083 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:47 records: The future mobile application must be fully native React Native/Expo with no WebView. Begin only after browser and warehouse approval; test in Expo before any final APK/AAB build.
011084 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:49 records: ## 34–38. Troubleshooting, commands, glossary, timeline, roadmap
011085 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:51 records: If a migration/reset proof is stale, stop writers, inspect again, create a new backup, and repeat the disposable test. If integrity or foreign keys fail, do not reset or restore automatically. If a scan is ambiguous, confirm account and identifier source. If a task is busy, retry through the supported UI rather than editing the database. If private storage is missing, restore the matching database and storage snapshot together after reviewing newer-data loss.
011086 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:53 records: Core commands are documented in `README.md`, `docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md`, `docs/FRESH_START_DATABASE_RESET.md`, and `docs/FRESH_START_QA_PLAN.md`. Important terms: account (seller scope), listing (marketplace catalog row), identifier (exact lookup value), route (ordered stages), task (one stage), consignment (marketplace shipment batch), snapshot (activation-time catalog evidence), idempotency (safe replay), and fresh start (one owner, no operational rows, unchanged schema/migra
011087 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:55 records: The evidence-backed commit timeline and phase detail are in `docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md`. The roadmap retains manual QA, stronger-hardware performance, production rollout approval, native Expo work, and final APK packaging as separate gates.
011088 | Evidence catalogue docs/PROJECT_MASTER_GUIDE.md:57 records: The consolidated history requested a target of 50,000 lines. The clean-base evidence-backed result is 18,924 lines in `PROJECT_HISTORY_PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_18924_LINES.txt`, generated strictly from commit `2981db0` plus this reset checkpoint. The repository contains only enough distinct verified evidence for that count; padding and later-branch evidence were rejected. Accuracy and branch purity take priority over the requested size.
011089 | Evidence catalogue docs/QA_PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKERS.md:1 records: # Phase 3 Consignment Worker QA
011090 | Evidence catalogue docs/QA_PHASE_3_CONSIGNMENT_WORKERS.md:3 records: Use fake or sanitized data only. Never commit uploaded files or database files.
