011178 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:3 records: This workflow preserves the current private SQLite data. Stop the website and all worker/import activity first. Never run `migrate reset`, `db push`, or seed against the real database.
011179 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:5 records: ## Prepare And Verify
011180 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:7 records: ```powershell
011181 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:9 records: npm.cmd run real-db:inspect

011185 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:15 records: Backups use Node SQLite's consistent backup API and are stored as timestamped files under ignored `backups/database/`. Each private manifest records source/backup sizes, SHA-256 hashes, integrity, commit, and migration state. The disposable migration test copy is always asserted inside `.codex-tmp/`.
011186 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:17 records: The copied database must pass migration deployment, migration status, integrity, foreign keys, preserved table/count/ID bounds, and targeted application suites. `real-db:verify` also proves the real database has not changed since backup.

011189 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:23 records: ```powershell

011191 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:27 records: The command displays the resolved path and requires typing the database filename. It refuses missing proofs or a changed source hash. Post-migration counts and comparison remain private in `.codex-tmp/`.
011192 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:29 records: ## Restore Manually
011193 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:31 records: Stop the server and every writer. Preserve the migrated database rather than overwriting it. Verify the selected backup manifest and SHA-256, then explicitly copy the verified backup to the configured database path using an owner-approved command. Run integrity, foreign-key, migration-status, and startup checks afterward.
011194 | Evidence catalogue docs/REAL_DATABASE_BACKUP_AND_MIGRATION.md:33 records: Never automate rollback: restoring a stale backup can erase work completed after migration.

011234 | Evidence catalogue docs/UNIVERSAL_WORK_SCANNER.md:9 records: 1. Scan or enter an AWB, Tracking ID, order code, SKU, FSN, listing ID, barcode, consignment number, or supported work-task ID.

011236 | Evidence catalogue docs/UNIVERSAL_WORK_SCANNER.md:11 records: 3. Review every exact active match. Account, marketplace, source, stage, assignment, and quantities are shown on each card.

011240 | Evidence catalogue docs/UNIVERSAL_WORK_SCANNER.md:17 records: Completed work is not actionable. When only completed matches exist, the page reports that matching work is already completed without presenting a completed card. A stale card is re-authorized and rejected safely; the worker scans again rather than replaying a mutation automatically.
011241 | Evidence catalogue docs/UNIVERSAL_WORK_SCANNER.md:19 records: For customer-order Assembly, required work appears as a Send to Assembly or Assembly task card. 011270 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:1 records: # Workflow Roadmap
011271 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:3 records: Marketplace Pick & Pack is a worker workflow system, not an ERP or inventory product.
011272 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:5 records: ## Current Live Flow
011273 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:7 records: - Ready-made customer order: Pick, then Pack.
011274 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:8 records: - Assembly-required customer order: Pick, Assembly task, then Pack.
011275 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:9 records: - Existing `Order` pick/pack statuses remain authoritative.
011276 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:11 records: ## Phase 1 Foundation
011277 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:13 records: - Generic listing identifiers.
011278 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:14 records: - Owner marking asset/file library.
011279 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:15 records: - Explicit multi-account listing links.
011280 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:16 records: - Product process routes.
011281 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:17 records: - Dormant generic `WorkTask` stage plans.
011282 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:18 records: - Mark/Assemble/management permissions.
011283 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:20 records: No existing order task rows are created.
011284 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:22 records: ## Phase 2: Owner Consignment Activation
011285 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:24 records: Implemented owner-side Flipkart consignment CSV/ZIP intake, safe supporting-file classification, account-scoped listing matching, process-route review, and explicit transactional activation. WorkTask source, quantity, and uniqueness constraints are enforced, but no consignment worker screen invokes task transitions yet.
011286 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:26 records: ## Phase 3: Consignment Worker Flow
011287 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:28 records: Implemented the worker Work Hub, Consignment Picker, Marking, Consignment Packing, stage-scoped exact search, atomic assignment claims, idempotent quantity progress, task problems, and line/batch completion reconciliation. Search never mutates work.
011288 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:30 records: ## Phase 4: Universal Cross-account Scanner
011289 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:32 records: Implemented one exact, lookup-only scanner for customer orders and active consignment Pick/Mark/Pack tasks across every authorized active seller account. `/work/scan` and `/packing` reuse the same resolver and cards. Multiple matches remain separate, completed work is non-actionable, and every explicit action re-authorizes its account and source.
011290 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:34 records: ## Phase 5: Simple Customer Order Assembly
011291 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:36 records: Implemented exact process-rule resolution, immutable Assembly task snapshots, automatic task creation after Pick, manual diversion, assignment/problem/owner-skip controls, an Assembly queue, scanner integration, and a shipment-wide transactional packing gate. This is task workflow only and introduces no inventory or manufacturing subsystem.
011292 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:38 records: ## Phase 6: Amazon Consignment And Rich Marking Cards
011293 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:40 records: Implemented bounded Amazon CSV/TSV/XLSX/XLSM/ZIP classification, account-scoped listing/catalog enrichment, exact FNSKU/SKU/ASIN/external/barcode matching, owner preview and activation, immutable catalog snapshots, shared Pick/Mark/Pack tasks, Amazon scanner identifiers, and rich marking details. Worker marking-file delivery is postponed while the owner library remains preserved.
011294 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:42 records: ## Phase 7: Performance And Complete Backend QA
011295 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:44 records: Added bounded Amazon stored-reparse policy, real-resolver scale presets and benchmarks, query-plan checks, controlled concurrency and replay hardening, a documented permission matrix, security source checks, and browser/warehouse release gates. Full-size generation is local and ignored; measured results are documented honestly per machine.
011296 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:46 records: ## Next Phases

011300 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md:52 records: Inventory balances, branch/warehouse stock, receiving, QC, stock deductions, valuation, reservations, in-transit inventory, and marketplace stock updates are explicitly excluded.
011301 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_ASSIGNMENT.md:1 records: # Work Task Assignment
011302 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_ASSIGNMENT.md:3 records: Workers may act only in active assigned accounts and only on permitted stages. Unassigned READY work is visible to eligible workers; the first progress action claims it atomically. A simultaneous second claimant receives a taken-work response and cannot add progress.
011303 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_ASSIGNMENT.md:5 records: Assigned work is mutable by its assignee or an owner. Managers can assign, unassign, and reassign current or locked future stages only to active account users with the required permission. A preassigned locked task keeps that assignment when unlocked; otherwise the next stage remains unassigned. Every administrative assignment change is written to `WorkActionLog`.
011304 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_ASSIGNMENT.md:7 records: All progress requests carry the expected quantity and may carry a unique client request ID. Stale quantities are rejected and repeated IDs return the original result without applying a second increment.
011305 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_PROBLEMS.md:1 records: # Work Task Problems
011306 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_PROBLEMS.md:3 records: Workers report a category and optional note from a task card. The task keeps its completed quantity and assignment, changes to PROBLEM, blocks progress, and leaves later stages locked. The batch becomes PROBLEM while any task remains problematic.
011307 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_PROBLEMS.md:5 records: Owners and consignment managers use `/work/problems` to resolve or reassign work. Resolution requires a note, preserves the original reason and action history, and returns zero-progress tasks to READY or partial tasks to IN_PROGRESS. After the final problem is resolved, reconciliation restores the batch to ACTIVE unless all final PACK work is complete.
011308 | Evidence catalogue docs/WORK_TASK_PROBLEMS.md:7 records: Problems are operational history and are never deleted by resolution.

011325 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:1 records: # Cloudflare Tunnel Safety Setup
011326 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:3 records: Cloudflare Tunnel is optional. It gives workers an HTTPS/domain URL without opening router ports.
011327 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:5 records: ## How It Works
011328 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:7 records: - Cloudflare Tunnel runs on the owner/server PC.
011329 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:8 records: - The tunnel points to `http://localhost:3000`.
011330 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:9 records: - Workers open `https://pack.personalizedgiftday.com`.
011331 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:10 records: - App login is still required for every picker, packer, and owner page.
011332 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:11 records: - Cached product images are protected by the app session and account checks.
011333 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:13 records: ## Daily Tunnel Flow
011334 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:15 records: 1. Start the app with `scripts/windows/start-meesho-app.bat`.
011335 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:16 records: 2. Start the tunnel:
011336 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:18 records: ```powershell
011337 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:19 records: cloudflared tunnel run meesho-pick-pack
011338 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:22 records: 3. Workers open:
011339 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:25 records: https://pack.personalizedgiftday.com
011340 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:28 records: ## Local Wi-Fi Testing
011341 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:30 records: Cloudflare is not required for same-Wi-Fi testing.
011342 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:35 records: http://<PC-IP>:3000
011343 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:38 records: For local HTTP, set:
011344 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:41 records: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000
011345 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:42 records: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=false
011346 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:45 records: ## Cloudflare HTTPS Mode
011347 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:47 records: For the production tunnel URL, set:
011348 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:50 records: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://pack.personalizedgiftday.com
011349 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:51 records: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=true
011350 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:54 records: ## Owner Security Checklist
011351 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:56 records: - Use strong owner, picker, and packer passwords.
011352 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:57 records: - Remove or change all demo users/passwords.
011353 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:58 records: - Owner can deactivate/reactivate users from **Owner -> Users**.
011354 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:59 records: - Owner can reset passwords without seeing plaintext passwords.
011355 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:60 records: - Owner can force password change on next login.
011356 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:61 records: - Owner can close active sessions for unknown devices.
011357 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:64 records: Recommended future hardening:
011358 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:66 records: - Enable Cloudflare Access for allowed owner/worker emails.
011359 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:67 records: - Keep Cloudflare and Windows updated.
011360 | Evidence catalogue docs/cloudflare-tunnel/security-setup.md:68 records: - Review **Owner -> System** and audit logs regularly.

011448 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:1 records: # Flipkart Real Export Test Guide
011449 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:7 records: ## Listing Excel
011450 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:9 records: For the first real-like Listing Master test:

011466 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:31 records: - Replace `Shipment ID` with fake values such as `TESTSHIP0001`.
011467 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:32 records: - Replace `Tracking ID` with fake values such as `FMPC0000000001`.
011468 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:33 records: - Keep SKU real only when matching Listing Master, or replace it consistently in both files.
011469 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:36 records: Daily workers should upload only Flipkart Order Excel after Listing Master is already imported.
011470 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:40 records: Run this before importing in the browser:
011471 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:46 records: The dry run does not require a database. It prints row counts, duplicate counts, missing required fields, missing listing/image warnings, unique SKUs, unique Tracking IDs, multi-item Tracking IDs, unknown headers, and missing expected headers.
011472 | Evidence catalogue docs/flipkart-real-test-guide.md:48 records: ## Manual Test Order

011479 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/ARCHITECTURE_AND_DATA_MODEL.md:1 records: # Architecture and data-model history
011480 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/ARCHITECTURE_AND_DATA_MODEL.md:3 records: The repository evolved from a Next.js owner-PC tool into an account-scoped catalog and staged-work system. SQLite is authoritative for local deployment; Prisma supplies equivalent SQLite and PostgreSQL models/migrations. UI code calls server actions/services, and exact account/capability checks must occur at the server boundary.
011481 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/ARCHITECTURE_AND_DATA_MODEL.md:5 records: Core identity models are User, UserDeviceSession, PasswordResetRequest, Account, the implicit assigned-account join, and AuditLog. Catalog models are MarketplaceListing, MarketplaceListingIdentifier, SkuImageMapping, ProductProcessRule, MarkingAsset, MarkingAssetFile, and MarkingAssetListingLink. Import models are ImportJob, UploadBatch, ImportRowIssue, and UploadPreviewRow. Operational models are Order, ProblemOrder, ScanLog, ConsignmentBatch, ConsignmentLine, ConsignmentImportFile, Consignment
011482 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/ARCHITECTURE_AND_DATA_MODEL.md:7 records: Account foreign keys provide tenant-like separation on one owner-controlled database. WorkTask links either an Order or ConsignmentLine and records source, stage, sequence, quantities, status, assignment, actors, problem state, and timestamps. WorkActionLog records immutable action/replay evidence. Consignment lines retain source values and activation snapshots so later catalog edits do not rewrite warehouse evidence.
011483 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/ARCHITECTURE_AND_DATA_MODEL.md:9 records: Fresh-start reset classification is deliberately schema-derived. `_prisma_migrations` is preserved; User preserves one selected OWNER; every other application table is deleted. Foreign keys determine child-before-parent order, and post-reset verification rejects any nonzero operational table.

011486 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/BUGS_FIXES_AND_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md:4 records: - Customer-order packing was aligned with the shared shipment-safety rule in Phase 4.2.
011487 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/BUGS_FIXES_AND_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md:5 records: - Amazon parsing and snapshot scanning received two Phase 6 correction commits.
011488 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/BUGS_FIXES_AND_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md:6 records: - Unsupported consignment assembly activation was blocked in Phase 7.2A.
011489 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/BUGS_FIXES_AND_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md:7 records: - Phase 7 review approved automated/code work but clarified that the benchmark result is small-only, query plans are representative, and high concurrency is quantity-increment-specific.
011490 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/BUGS_FIXES_AND_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md:8 records: - Nonblocking backend cleanup remains for older claim/problem/reassignment replay callbacks or narrower documentation.

011493 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/DATABASE_MIGRATION_AND_BACKUP_HISTORY.md:1 records: # Database migration and backup history
011494 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/DATABASE_MIGRATION_AND_BACKUP_HISTORY.md:3 records: Schema evolution uses ordered additive or reviewed safe-copy migrations, maintained separately for SQLite and PostgreSQL. Migration smokes protect fresh and existing-style databases. Real-data migration tooling introduced inspect, consistent backup, SHA-256, copied deployment, preserved counts/identity bounds, unchanged-source proof, explicit confirmation, and post-verification.
011495 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/DATABASE_MIGRATION_AND_BACKUP_HISTORY.md:5 records: Fresh-start is not a migration reset. It preserves the database file, schema objects, and every applied `_prisma_migrations` row. A backup captures database and active storage under one timestamp. A disposable reset proves owner/hash/migration preservation and zero operational counts. The real command refuses stale proofs and requires exact database, owner, and deletion phrase input.
011496 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/DATABASE_MIGRATION_AND_BACKUP_HISTORY.md:7 records: Restore is manual because replacing a database or storage tree can erase newer work. The helper verifies the chosen manifest, makes a pre-restore database safety copy, restores the database, and checks integrity/foreign keys. Storage is restored only after an owner decision.

011508 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/IMPORT_AND_CATALOG_HISTORY.md:1 records: # Import and catalog history
011509 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/IMPORT_AND_CATALOG_HISTORY.md:3 records: Early uploads created orders and SKU/image mappings. Flipkart added account-scoped listing master and consignment imports. Amazon added workbook/archive classification, candidate worksheet policy, catalog matching, and snapshot enrichment. ImportJob later represented durable background progress and recovery.
011510 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/IMPORT_AND_CATALOG_HISTORY.md:5 records: MarketplaceListing is the current catalog center; MarketplaceListingIdentifier enables normalized exact lookup. Imports merge by marketplace identity rules and retain warnings/errors instead of silently guessing. Optional ProductProcessRule selects a route and marking asset; no rule means Pick-Pack.
011511 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/IMPORT_AND_CATALOG_HISTORY.md:7 records: Amazon reference worksheets are excluded by default. Stored reparsing has limits for file count, aggregate/individual bytes, cells, and archive-derived entries, and submitted worksheet selection is revalidated server-side. Flipkart multi-item shipment safety remains part of packing, not merely parsing.
011512 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/IMPORT_AND_CATALOG_HISTORY.md:9 records: Phase 7.2B is not implemented on this checkpoint branch. No later bulk-refresh route, migration, catalog-merge service, or test is included in this history. Phase 7.2C is also not implemented.

011534 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:22 records: ## Per-phase concerns
011535 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:24 records: Phase 1 introduced account-scoped marking/catalog/workflow models and permissions without replacing existing order pick/pack truth. Owner flows manage marking assets and optional rules; workers consume authorized tasks and files. Additive SQLite/PostgreSQL migrations and marking migration smoke tests protect existing data.
011536 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:26 records: Phase 2 added Flipkart consignment batch, line, file, and issue data; parse/review/match/activate owner flows; immutable activation snapshots; and task creation. Imports are account-scoped and activation requires reviewed valid lines. Parser, foundation, integration, and migration tests cover the path.
011537 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:28 records: Phase 3 exposed staged consignment work for authorized workers. Assignment, claim, progress, marking access, completion, and problems became operational. Phase 3.1 added explicit request kind/ID isolation, permission matrices, replay rules, competing claimant tests, duplicate problem/resolution behavior, and assignment controls.
011538 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:30 records: Phase 4 unified exact scanning across accounts and work sources. Phase 4.1 corrected shipment aggregation and safe transitions. Phase 4.2 ensured customer packing uses the same shipment-safety rule instead of a weaker isolated check. Authorization remains server-side and result categories distinguish active/completed/none.
011539 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:32 records: Phase 5 added Assembly for customer orders, ordered route transitions, assembly capability checks, and dedicated tests. Consignment assembly was not proven and later became an explicit blocked route.
011540 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:34 records: Phase 6 extended consignments to Amazon, catalog/snapshot identifiers (ASIN, FNSKU, external ID, barcode), stored source candidates, archive safety, and rich marking data. Corrections centralized worksheet candidate policy, revalidated selections server-side, and completed snapshot scanning evidence.
011541 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:36 records: Phase 7 added small/medium/large/full synthetic presets, including 800,000-listing configurations. Only the small full-resolver benchmark was reported. Six representative exact query plans were checked. Duplicate quantity increments received 2/5/10/20 contention coverage; other action families retained narrower tests. Quantity mutation gained bounded retries and replay recovery, while some older claim/problem/reassignment callbacks retain transaction-based reads.
011542 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:38 records: Phase 7.1 introduced safe handling for the private SQLite database: inspect, consistent hash-verified backup, copied migration test, unchanged-source verification, explicit migration confirmation, post-checks, and a read-only manual review page. It avoids reset/push/seed operations against real data.
011543 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:40 records: Phase 7.2 established MarketplaceListing as Product Inventory and ProductProcessRule as optional defaults. Phase 7.2A prevents activation of unsupported consignment assembly routes. Product Inventory remains catalog data, not stock.

Phase 7.2B is not implemented on this branch, and Phase 7.2C is not implemented. Later-branch routes, migrations, services, tests, documents, and claims are excluded.
011546 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:46 records: Production remains gated by manual browser widths, sanitized warehouse QA, backup/migration/reset review, and medium-or-larger performance on stronger hardware. Native Expo and APK work remain later gates.
011547 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:48 records: ## Consolidated evidence count
011548 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_HISTORY.md:50 records: The requested target was 50,000 lines. The clean-base evidence-backed result is 18,924 lines in `PROJECT_HISTORY_PHASE_1_TO_7_2A_18924_LINES.txt`. 011549 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/SECURITY_AND_CONCURRENCY_HISTORY.md:1 records: # Security and concurrency history
011550 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/SECURITY_AND_CONCURRENCY_HISTORY.md:3 records: Security layers include hashed-password authentication, active/lockout state, device sessions, reset requests, audit logs, exact role/capability checks, account scoping, input validation, safe path handling, upload/parser/archive limits, and private ignored operational files.
011551 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/SECURITY_AND_CONCURRENCY_HISTORY.md:5 records: Idempotency uses task, actor, request kind, and client request identity. Duplicate quantity requests must yield one mutation and one action log. Bounded retry handles transient SQLite contention and safe replay recovery converts a committed competing request into its prior result. Controlled busy errors avoid leaking low-level database failures.
011552 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/SECURITY_AND_CONCURRENCY_HISTORY.md:7 records: The reset checkpoint adds exact owner validation, read-only inspection, consistent SQLite backup, SHA-256 verification, migration checksum/name comparison, unchanged-source proof, disposable-copy proof, three typed confirmations, transaction verification, and nonautomatic restore. Password hashes, tokens, and private business rows are never printed or documented.
011553 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/TEST_VALIDATION_AND_QA_HISTORY.md:1 records: # Test, validation, and QA history
011554 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/TEST_VALIDATION_AND_QA_HISTORY.md:3 records: Parser and validation suites cover Meesho, Flipkart, Amazon, import progress, marking, consignments, workflows, scanning, assembly, and source-policy constraints. Migration smoke tests apply migrations to fresh and existing-style disposable SQLite databases. Integration tests cover claims, problems, resolution, assignment, duplicates, stage unlocking, completion, and cross-account rules.
011555 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/TEST_VALIDATION_AND_QA_HISTORY.md:5 records: Phase 7 adds a permission matrix, security checks, contention tests, real resolver benchmark, and query plans. Test reporting must distinguish generator capacity from executed scale, representative queries from exhaustive plans, and increment contention from other action coverage.
011556 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/TEST_VALIDATION_AND_QA_HISTORY.md:7 records: Phase 7.1 adds real-database safety source tests and copied migration verification. The fresh-start checkpoint adds migration-complete synthetic reset tests and real-command refusal tests. Manual browser, warehouse, login, empty-state, restore, and stronger-hardware performance checks remain mandatory.
011557 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/WORKFLOW_AND_PERMISSION_HISTORY.md:1 records: # Workflow and permission history
011558 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/WORKFLOW_AND_PERMISSION_HISTORY.md:3 records: Workflow began with marking/process/task foundations, then added Flipkart activation and worker Pick/Mark/Pack, hardened request replay and permissions, unified scanning and packing safety, and added customer Assembly. Amazon later reused the consignment model with marketplace-specific parsing and snapshots.
011559 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/WORKFLOW_AND_PERMISSION_HISTORY.md:5 records: Capabilities are explicit: pick, pack, mark, assemble, report problem, manage marking library, manage process rules, view all work, view/import/manage consignments. Selected and assigned seller accounts constrain access. Owners manage configuration and imports; workers see and mutate only authorized stage/account work; view-all access does not imply mutation.
011560 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/WORKFLOW_AND_PERMISSION_HISTORY.md:7 records: Task progression is ordered. Locked future stages cannot start early. Problems preserve recoverable prior state. Assignments, claims, quantities, completion, and action logs are checked together. Client request identity prevents duplicate effects. Shipment-level packing checks prevent one line from being packed while related required work is incomplete.
011561 | Evidence catalogue docs/history/WORKFLOW_AND_PERMISSION_HISTORY.md:9 records: Known limitation: the high-contention 2/5/10/20 test applies specifically to duplicate quantity increments. Some other action families have two-request or targeted competition tests, and some replay callbacks still open read transactions.

011604 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:60 records: 2. Open **Pack**.
011605 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:61 records: 3. Type a full AWB and open the order.
011606 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:62 records: 4. Type only the last 5 to 8 AWB characters and confirm suggestions rank exact, suffix, then contains matches.
011607 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:63 records: 5. Use the camera scanner on the HTTPS Cloudflare URL.
011608 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:64 records: 6. Confirm the scanner stops and shows **Opening order...** after a valid AWB.
011609 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:65 records: 7. Confirm the packing result page shows SKU, quantity, color, courier, AWB, and cached image or placeholder.
011610 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:66 records: 8. Confirm packed.
011611 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:67 records: 9. Repeat confirm on the same order and verify it is safely treated as already packed.
011612 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:68 records: 10. Mark one order as a problem and verify it cannot be packed accidentally.
011613 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:70 records: ## Exports And Backup
011614 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:72 records: 1. Open **Owner -> System**.
011615 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:73 records: 2. Export orders, packed orders, pending orders, problem orders, scan logs, SKU mappings, and upload batches.
011616 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:75 records: 4. Confirm `storage/product-images/` is not committed to Git and can be regenerated from SKU image URLs.
011617 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:79 records: 1. Open **Owner -> Cleanup**.
011618 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:80 records: 2. Confirm image cache cleanup shows only images unused for 30+ days.
011619 | Evidence catalogue docs/manual-smoke-test.md:81 records: 3. Confirm cleanup requires the exact confirmation text before deleting files.
011620 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:1 records: # Mobile Local Connection Guide
011621 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:3 records: The future native Android app should connect to the owner PC/server running Marketplace Pick & Pack. Android should never connect directly to the database.
011622 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:5 records: ## Same Wi-Fi
011623 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:7 records: 1. Start the owner PC app on all network interfaces:
011624 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:9 records: ```powershell
011625 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:10 records: npm.cmd run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3001
011626 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:13 records: 2. Find the PC private IP address, for example:
011627 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:16 records: 192.168.1.10
011628 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:19 records: 3. In the Android app, set server URL:
011629 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:22 records: http://192.168.1.10:3001
011630 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:25 records: 4. Allow Windows Firewall access on the private network for Node.js / the app port.
011631 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:27 records: Use this only on trusted warehouse Wi-Fi.
011632 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:29 records: If `LOCAL_NETWORK_ONLY=true`, keep `TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS=false` for direct same-Wi-Fi access. Enable `TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS=true` only when a trusted proxy overwrites `X-Forwarded-For` / `X-Real-IP`; do not trust client-supplied forwarding headers.
011633 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:31 records: ## Different Wi-Fi Or Mobile Data
011634 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:33 records: Use a private VPN instead of exposing the database or router port publicly.
011635 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:35 records: Recommended options:
011636 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:42 records: 1. Install Tailscale or ZeroTier on the owner PC.
011637 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:43 records: 2. Install the same VPN on the Android phone.
011638 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:44 records: 3. Join both devices to the same private network.
011639 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:45 records: 4. Start the app on the owner PC at `0.0.0.0:3001`.
011640 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:46 records: 5. Set Android server URL to the private VPN IP:
011641 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:49 records: http://100.x.y.z:3001
011642 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:52 records: No public domain is required. Supabase is not required for local owner-PC mode.
011643 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:54 records: ## Safety Rules
011644 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:57 records: - Do not put database passwords in the Android app.
011645 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:59 records: - Do not port-forward the router unless the app is properly secured and intentionally exposed.
011646 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:60 records: - Private VPN access is recommended for phones outside the warehouse Wi-Fi.
011647 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:61 records: - Prefer Tailscale/ZeroTier private IP access over public router forwarding.
011648 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:63 records: ## Scanner Flow
011649 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:65 records: The native Android scanner reads the barcode on the phone and sends only the scanned code to:
011650 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:68 records: GET /api/mobile/packing/search?code=FMPC0000000000
011651 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:71 records: When the worker taps Pack, Android calls:
011652 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:74 records: POST /api/mobile/packing/confirm
011653 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-local-connection.md:77 records: The server decides what can be packed. The Android app does not update the database directly.
011654 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:1 records: # Mobile Native API Plan
011655 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:3 records: This app is being prepared for a future native Android warehouse app. The Android app must talk only to the local Marketplace Pick & Pack server API. It must never connect directly to SQLite, PostgreSQL, Supabase, or any database host.
011656 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:5 records: ## Architecture
011657 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:8 records: Android app -> local Next.js API -> Prisma/server code -> database on owner PC/server
011658 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:11 records: - The database stays on the owner PC or private server.
011659 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:13 records: - The Android app stores only the server URL and its authenticated session cookie/token.
011660 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:14 records: - No database password belongs in the Android APK, Android source code, Gradle config, or mobile settings screen.
011661 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:16 records: ## Authentication Flow
011662 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:18 records: 1. Android sends username and password to `POST /api/mobile/auth/login`.
011663 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:19 records: 2. The server verifies the password hash using existing backend password logic.
011664 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:20 records: 3. Disabled users and locked users are rejected by the server.
011665 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:21 records: 4. If the user must change password, the API returns `{ mustChangePassword: true }`.
011666 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:22 records: 5. On success, the server creates a secure server-side session and returns safe user/account data.
011667 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:23 records: 6. Android sends later API requests to the same local server with the session cookie.
011668 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:24 records: 7. `POST /api/mobile/auth/logout` clears the session.
011669 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:26 records: The mobile response never includes password hash, password salt, session secret, database URL, or raw private data.
011670 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:28 records: ## Role And Account Scope
011671 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:30 records: Every mobile endpoint must enforce these rules on the server:
011672 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:32 records: - User must be logged in and active.
011673 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:33 records: - `mustChangePassword` users cannot use worker APIs until password change is complete.
011674 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:34 records: - Owner can access all active accounts.
011675 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:35 records: - Picker and packer can access only assigned active accounts.
011676 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:36 records: - `accountId` from Android is only a selector. It is never authorization proof.
011677 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:37 records: - Worker data is scoped by role and assigned account.
011678 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:39 records: ## API Surface
011679 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:41 records: - `POST /api/mobile/auth/login`
011680 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:42 records: - `POST /api/mobile/auth/logout`
011681 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:43 records: - `GET /api/mobile/me`
011682 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:44 records: - `GET /api/mobile/picker/groups`
011683 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:45 records: - `POST /api/mobile/picker/mark-picked`
011684 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:46 records: - `POST /api/mobile/picker/problem`
011685 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:47 records: - `GET /api/mobile/packing/search`
011686 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:48 records: - `POST /api/mobile/packing/confirm`
011687 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:49 records: - `POST /api/mobile/packing/problem`
011688 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:50 records: - `GET /api/mobile/products/[sku]/images`
011689 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:51 records: - `GET /api/mobile/products/[sku]/details`
011690 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:52 records: - `GET /api/mobile/sync/status`
011691 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:54 records: ## Fast Worker Data
011692 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:56 records: Picker group list returns compact fields only:
011693 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:61 records: - pending/picked/problem counts
011694 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:62 records: - color and size
011695 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:63 records: - main image URL
011696 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:64 records: - cache status
011697 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:67 records: The picker list must not return full description, all specifications, all listings, customer address, phone, password hash, password salt, or database internals.
011698 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:69 records: Product details are separate at `GET /api/mobile/products/[sku]/details` so heavy listing fields load only when the worker opens Details.
011699 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:71 records: ## Packing And Scanner Flow
011700 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:73 records: Native Android scanner reads the barcode locally, then sends the scanned value to:
011701 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:76 records: GET /api/mobile/packing/search?code=FMPC0000000000
011702 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:79 records: The API searches Tracking ID first, then AWB/internal keys. If one Flipkart Tracking ID has multiple SKUs, the API returns all matching shipment items. The Android app shows the result and calls:
011703 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:82 records: POST /api/mobile/packing/confirm
011704 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:85 records: Packing confirmation packs only `READY` items, skips already packed items, leaves problem items as problem, and writes server-side audit/scan logs.
011705 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:87 records: Native Android scanning does not need browser camera HTTPS permissions because the scanner runs inside the native app and only sends the scanned text to the API.
011706 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:89 records: ## Network Model
011707 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:91 records: Same Wi-Fi is allowed for local warehouse use. For different Wi-Fi/mobile networks, use a private VPN such as Tailscale or ZeroTier.
011708 | Evidence catalogue docs/mobile-native-api-plan.md:93 records: Plain public router port forwarding is not recommended. If remote access is required, prefer a private VPN or a properly protected tunnel with strong authentication.
